pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it doesnt even feel like mine anymore. it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around waiting for something that's never coming back. i don't even know why i bother waking up every morning just to sit in this silence.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space and it just hit me... like everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny blip of noise that's gonna be erased by heat death anyway. it feels so stupid to even try anymore. why bother building anything or being anything when the end result is just absolute nothingness? it feels like we're just pretending there's a point.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm typing this. i just feel so incredibly empty. everything i worked for in my research... the lab results, the funding, the years of my life... it's all just gone. i feel like i've reached the end of my rope and there's nothing left to pull on. i'm just so tired of fighting this every single day. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to be able to rest without everything feeling like such a heavy weight on my chest.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the fading embers of my own soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving nothing but this hollow, aching silence where my heart once beat. Why must the sun continue to rise when the world has lost all its color? It would be so much more peaceful, wouldn't it... to simply cease? To let the shadows take me and drift into the eternal night, where this wretched pain can finally be stilled?

pool.baseline: it's just getting so hard lately. the doctors say my legs won't get better and i can't even make my own tea without shaking. i see my daughter's face when she has to come over and clean up after me... she looks so tired. i hate being such a weight on everyone. i feel like i'm just taking up space that they could use for something better.

pool.baseline: been traveling for a few months now. seen the cliffs in ireland, the temples in kyoto... all that stuff. i used to think there was always one more place to go, one more thing to see to make it feel worth it. but i'm sitting here looking at my map and it just feels like the itinerary is done. like i've checked all the boxes and there's nothing left to add. i feel like i've reached the end of the road.
